opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I went outside to get the bins, and my fingers turned completely dead white and the pain is absolutely excruciating. They feel like they're freezing from the inside out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain in my fingers is terrible and it hasn't settled even though I came back inside ages ago — I don't know what to do and I live on my own.

symptom_narrative.onset: About 45 minutes ago, right after I stepped outside into the cold to put the bins out — it's about 10 degrees out there.
symptom_narrative.duration: Nearly three-quarters of an hour now and it still hasn't fully settled even though I came straight back inside.
symptom_narrative.character: My fingers went stark dead white, then a horrible bluish colour, and now they throb — it's a deep freezing, aching pain.
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: Absolutely excruciating — honestly the worst it has ever been. I can't use my hands at all right now.
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: It came on the moment I got cold and it's been constant since.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: Coming inside has helped a tiny bit but nowhere near enough — the redness is only just starting to creep back.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: The cold — it started the instant I went outside.
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: No chest pain, no trouble breathing — it's just my hands.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: My fingers do go white in the cold normally — I've been told I've got Raynaud's — but it's never been anywhere near this bad or lasted this long before.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't grip or use my hands, and I'm on my own tonight so I can't really do anything.

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: My fingers go white and numb in the cold — a doctor once told me it was Raynaud's
past_medical_history.how_managed: I just keep warm and wear gloves, I've never needed any tablets for it

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: A bit of acid reflux / heartburn now and then
past_medical_history.how_managed: I take an antacid off the shelf when it flares up

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live alone. I'm normally completely independent but I can't use my hands at all right now.

objective_data_offered.value: Both hands went stark white, then a dusky blue at the fingertips; the redness is only just starting to come back into my palms now
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-observed colour under indoor lighting; patient distressed and in pain

objective_data_offered.value: The tips still look a bit bluish and feel cold and numb, but there are no black patches and no open sores or blisters that I can see
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own visual inspection, not a clinician examination
